market_context: Markets face multiple headwinds with Iran war keeping oil near $100, Fed PCE inflation data due at 1:30 PM, and private credit redemption concerns weighing on financials. Pre-market futures show modest recovery attempt with SPY up 0.3% after yesterday's 1.5% decline to lowest levels since November.

predictions.reasoning: Despite pre-market bounce, PCE inflation data at 1:30 PM likely to show persistent inflation amid Iran war energy spike. University of Michigan sentiment expected at gloomy 55 vs 56.6 prior. Multiple headwinds including Fed rate cut expectations fading and GDP revision lower than expected.

Tech rebound likely ahead of NVIDIA GTC conference starting Monday. Chip stocks showing strength pre-market on Oracle/TSMC optimism. QQQ found support at 593 level and positioning ahead of major AI conference catalyst should drive buying interest despite broader market weakness.

Strong positioning trade ahead of GTC 2026 conference starting Monday March 16. Wall Street overwhelmingly bullish with 38 Strong Buy ratings and $273 average target. Daiwa reiterated $215 target ahead of GTC. Historical pattern shows NVDA outperforms 30% in three months following GTC events.

Oil prices pulling back today after U.S. temporarily removed sanctions on Russian oil to expand supply. WTI futures retreated 1% to around $94 after hitting $95.73 yesterday. Treasury Secretary's comments about 'narrowly tailored, short-term measure' on Russian oil should pressure energy stocks.

Private credit fund redemption issues continue to weigh on Morgan Stanley. The North Haven Private Income Fund received redemption requests totaling 10.9% of shares but can only honor 45.8% of requests. MS already fell 3.6% Thursday on these concerns and pressure likely continues.
